entry.title: Filter on Employee centeral one time payment
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m working on a scenario involving SAP SuccessFactors One-Time Payments, and I’d appreciate your insights on a potential workaround we're evaluating.

Context

  • The customer maintains an Assignment Number in SAP HCM IT0015.

  • This Assignment Number will be replicated from HCM to Employee Central, and populated into the customString1 field of the EmpPayCompNonRecurring entity.

  • The customer uses this Assignment Number to group multiple one-time payments.

  • A custom application reads one-time payment records from SuccessFactors, using this Assignment Number as the key for grouping.

The Problem

  • In the EmpPayCompNonRecurring entity, the field customString1 is not filterable via the OData API.

  • This limitation prevents us from using queries like:

    http
     
    $filter=customString1 eq '10000069'

  • The only available field that is filterable in this entity is sequenceNumber, which is a standard field.

Proposed Workaround

  • Create a business rule on the One-Time Payment “onSave” event.

  • Use this rule to populate the sequenceNumber field with:

    • The Assignment Number (from customString1),

    • Concatenated with a unique identifier to ensure each record remains distinct.

  • This would enable OData queries like:

    http
     
    $filter=startswith(sequenceNumber, '10000069')

    which would effectively retrieve all one-time payments grouped by Assignment Number.

Customer Concern

  • The customer is hesitant to use sequenceNumber in this way, since it is a standard field.

  • They are concerned that modifying it could introduce side effects or conflict with existing or future system behaviour.

Although I haven't found any technical issues with this approach so far, the concern is valid and understandable.

Questions for the Community

  • Has anyone implemented something similar to group or filter one-time payments using Assignment Number?

  • Have you ever overridden sequenceNumber for custom logic?

  • Are there any known side effects or caveats of using(modifying) standard fields like sequenceNumber this way?

  • Should we consider raising a ticket to SAP requesting enhancement of the One-Time Payment OData API to make fields like customString1 and customDouble1 filterable?

Looking forward to your thoughts and recommendations!

Thanks in advance,
Manu

entry.title: Re: Solved - How to join QUT1 and QUT10 tables?
entry.content:
Hi Fabio,

I think you forgot the OrderNum in your VisOrder. It is possible that there is more than one Textline. So you have to order it, too

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: Vertical scrollbar jumps to end.
entry.content:
Hi, when we click on check box, it calls handle event method where it manipulate checkbox (eg:- select or deselect based on other checkbox.....etc). Inside this method before manipulation, we are calling "get_channel_state" and after manipulation called "set_channel_state", this two method were called to get and set channel structure so that we can avoid collapse of alv tree. Now my doubt is weather scroll behaviour is controlled by standard or there is any method which control the behaviour of scrollbar or how it is associated with select/deselect of checkbox event??  @Sandra_Rossi Thank You.

Sample code snippet.

entry.title: Re: Vertical scrollbar jumps to end.

First Content Modifier and then Request Reply. 

Can you share the iflow details? what all steps needed and what all we need to pass in each step?  

Thanks

Gopal

entry.title: Re: Duplicate file check in sFTP adapter BTP cloud integr...
entry.content: Either you have to set a lookup in CPI to sort of check where the list of file names are recorded or just let the files through and have ABAP do the duplicate check for processing. We did the latter on my previous project.

entry.title: sapgui 800 Patch 6 - error 12007 despite xml file is accessible
entry.content:
hi all,

we have rolled out SAPGUI 800 PL 6 and getting reports from some users that when they start the SAPGUI app, they will get an error stating that the centrally placed xml config file cannot be read, despite there is no issue with reaching the xml file from the affected user machine. In most cases, when confirming the error message with "OK" , all is working as expected and systems are shown in the system overview section.

Does anyone else have a similar issue and an idea on how to get this resolved? During the testing this issue was not captured,  and I cannot find anything related to that in any SAPnotes (in regards to error 12007 and sapgui 800).

Any help is highly appreciated.

Best regards

alex

entry.title: Re: The results of the data action "XXXXXXX" could not be...
entry.content:
@saikhancs3524 

The error comes when the tenant does not have enough memory available to publish the data records generated by the Data Action. Happens when Data Action created lot of data records. You could try few steps below

  • Try running the Data Action for a limited scope. For e.g. 1 month or 1 company code etc.
  • Assign the correct planning area settings for the Data Action. You could use Empty Planning Area and see if that works. You can find the option on right pane of the Data Action starter settings.

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

In T-code me23n when "send object with note" is used, notification is sent in sap business workplace.
How can this notification be sent in my inbox or push notification in Fiori as well?

entry.title: external definition imported from sap pi is causing issue in sap cpi
entry.content:
hello,

we are migrating sap pi interfaces to sap cpi. after migrating interfaces which had external definition used in sap pi for source or target structure, we see in sap cpi in message mapping the structure shows missing node .may be due to prefix?

Is there any standard solution to fix this kind of issues as we have many pi interfaces to migrate to cpi and most o them use external definition in sap pi

 

thanks,

Sahana

entry.title: Issue with Time Event Pairing "P01" Over Weekends in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently encountering an issue related to time event pairing in SuccessFactors.

Our terminal system only sends "P01" time events to SuccessFactors, which requires that each event be paired with another of the same time type. However, we are experiencing a problem during weekends: the last time event on Friday is not being paired correctly, as the next time event occurs more than 24 hours later (typically on Monday). This time gap prevents the system from recognizing and pairing the events properly.

To address this, we considered creating a business rule that would automatically generate a manual time event (specifically a stop event) to close the open pair. However, we have not been successful in implementing this solution so far.

Has anyone faced a similar issue, or does anyone have suggestions or best practices for resolving this?

We would greatly appreciate your guidance or recommendations.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: New SAP Build Process Automation content for side-by-side extensions with flexible workflows
entry.content:
My colleagues published a SAP Build Process Automation action package "Flexible Workflow Scenario Notifications Action" on SAP Business Accelerator Hub and in the SAP Build Store so that it becomes easier for Citizen Developer to discover and consume it.

It oﬀers an action for an outcome driven extension workﬂow and makes it easier to combine ﬂexible workﬂows from SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build Process Automation capabilities.

From a use case perspective, you could think of including process participants from outside (like external project managers) into the SAP S/4HANA Process. Or you include centrally managed services from SAP BTP like a Rules Service or Joule Skills into your SAP S/4HANA workﬂow project. Or you like to leverage ML services like the recommendation capability of SAP Task Center, which is only available for SAP Build Process Automation tasks? For the blog I took the use case with process participants from outside to explain the possibilities.

In line with side-by-side extensibility ﬂexible workﬂow oﬀers an activitytype "extension step" which can be implemented by flexible workflow scenarios to offer side-by-side extension. It is guideline for all scenario developers to enable it.

I will give a short overview and add some points in addition to the oﬃcial documentation.

The solution architecture gives an overview on the connection between Sales Order Creation in SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP. Over the ﬂexible workﬂow capability of SAP Business Workﬂow it is possible to start an extension process and get back the result / outcome from SAP Build Process Automation .

Configuration in SAP S/4HANA starts with definition of decision reasons for sales orders. In our use case we want to start a flexible workﬂow which is the internal approval solution. After setting an internal approval reason a BAdI checks after saving the Sales Order if an approval is necessary. If true a start event is thrown and checks if a workﬂow is deﬁned in the Manage Workﬂow Application for our scenario. If the scenario contains an extension step, the business process expert is able to configures an extension step with a process deﬁnition from SAP Build Process Automation from the connected BTP subaccount. During runtime when the flow comes to the extension, a SAP Build Process Automation workflow is started via api and a standardize payload.

In the extension workﬂow of our use case we derive the complete sales order header data over an API call to SAP S/4HANA and use this data for Agent Determination. After agent determination via decision table a user decision is modelled with two outcomes.

After the user decision took place we need to set the outcome via script and use it in the action content package. Go to SAP Build Store. Search for "flexible".

Click on the action project for review the details.

Add the action to your project.

To use the action and point to the correct ABAP-Backend-System:

  1. define a destination variable in the SAP Build Process Automation project.
  2. define a destination in the BTP Cockpit to the ABAP-Backend-System(s)
  3. map the destination to the destination variable during deployment of the project.

Define the workflowInstanceId in the context and fill it from the start payload. For the names of the outcomes you need to look up the flexible workflow scenario or check the documentation of the used scenario. If it is custom build in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition or SAP S/4HANA (like in this blog) check your implementation of the extension step and fill the outcomes accordingly.

Please be aware that the development livecycle of the projects on SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP is independent. We work on further enhancements to simplify the integration via the extensibility wizard and also on more features to enrich the possibilities. So stay tuned. A big thanks to all colleagues supporting the integration.

entry.title: SAP BTP SDK for iOS incompatibility issues with Swift 6 (Xcode 16)
entry.content:
Hi SAP community.

I am facing some issues with the SAP SDK with Swift 6. Swift 6 is mainly about concurrency changes in Swift which does not seem to play along with the SAPFoundation XCFramework.

It seems like the SAPFoundation framework internally manages its own dispatch queues (specifically the SAP.Foundation.Authentication.WebViewAuthenticator.SingleAccess queue).

If a function uses this queue and is called within a MainActor or Task context, no matter if it is isolated or not, this will cause a crash.

An example of this is the following method:
SAMLAuthenticator.authenticate(session: _, completionHandler: _)

To reproduce the issue one can enable the Swift 6 language mode, initialize a SAMLAuthenticator and try to call authenticate in a Task { … } context or from a @MainActor context.

SAP BTP SDK version: 25.4.4
Xcode version: 16.4

Are there any known solutions to this issue?

entry.title: Custom Header in CPI
entry.content:
Hi, I added a script in my iFlow to add custom headers in MPL - I'm using the standard script however the custom header still shows in the message log even though the property has no value or equals to null. 

 The "po_property" stores a value from a local variable (which I deleted in this scenario) so basically it's considered as null? (correct me if I'm wrong)

Here's my script:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    
	def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message);
	if(messageLog != null){

		def po_number = message.getHeaders().get("po_number");		
		if(po_number!=null){
			messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty("Local Variable Test", po_number);		
        }
	}
	return message;
}

Has anyone encountered this or did I miss something?

entry.title: Logic for calculation of the actual coverage in the "Period total" view of MD04 screen
entry.content:
Hi All,
I want to understand the system logic of calculating the actual coverage in the "Period total" view of MD04 screen.
There is no coverage profile assigned in material master. If no coverage profile is assigned in material master how it calculates actual coverage which I am able to see in - MD04 -> Period Totals -> Actual Coverage (In days).

For Example:

Requirements-525.400,000-

Available quantity- 288.012,021

Actual coverage - 15,5

 

Requirements-58.228,000-

Available quantity- 578.188,021

Actual coverage - 34,2

Please let me know if you have any information.
Thanks.

entry.title: GRC BRM - Digital signature enablement Option is not seen
entry.content:
Dear SAP community, We are currently implementing BRM (Business Role Management) for a client and are looking to enable digital signature functionality during workflow approvals — specifically, we require approvers to enter their user ID and password when approving requests in their work inbox (similar to how it functions in ARM - Access Request Management).

However, we are unable to locate the "Digital Signature Enablement" option within BRM configuration. Could you please advise on how we can achieve the similar functionality in BRM. Please share the SAP note or configuration steps if available.

Thank you.

Can anyone suggest an efficient and reliable approach to handle this?

 

Thanks,
Smriti

entry.title: Re: Output tables in Kmeans
entry.content:
@Frengo, it is somewhat not transparent to me / the community how your app / scenarios looks in detail, which tools you use etc.

From the error code you shared, one idea about you problem is that in your procedure code, you use references to temporary tables which might not exist anymore by the time you execute the procedure: Can not insert data from temp table

entry.title: How to use OBH1/OBH2 on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear Experts.

I have a lot of companies, document types.

I want to use Tr-cd:OBH1/OBH2 on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition.

If I can't these PG, I can't maintain the number range intervals. Too many maintenance...

Please help.

entry.title: Re: In VA01/VA02, the user exit USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT ca...
entry.content:
Hi,

Modifying  XVBAK within the USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT exit is generally too late, as this exit is triggered just before the commit and changes to XVBAK are no longer considered.

Instead, you can call a custom function module such as Z_UPDATE_VBAK_CUSTOM_FIELD using the IN UPDATE TASK statement. Within that function module, you can safely perform an UPDATE on the VBAK using BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE with extension in paramter to persist your custom field changes. See 143580 - Information on SD BAPIs and customer enhancement concept

Preferably call the external system before the USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT.

call function 'Z_UPDATE_VBAK_CUSTOM_FIELD' in UPDATE TASK
exporting
if_vbeln = xvbak-vbeln
if_custom_field = lf_value_from_external_system.

entry.title: System landscape directory on the specified remote server is outdated
entry.content:
Hi,
We are running CCC PL20H1 in our environment, and we have 2 Service Units :

1. Runs Hana - has ServiceLayer installed and registered in CCC
2. SQL - no ServiceLayer installed
I am trying to install a new ServiceLayer instance for the SQL SU but failing with "The version of the system landscape directory on the specified remote server is outdated. Before upgrading this computer, you must upgrade the system landscape directory on the remote machine."

 



Does anyone knows what might be the root cause?
Thank you

entry.title: Re: Not able to find accounting view in material creation.
entry.content: It depends on your material type configuration settings.  Go to OMS2, select your material type and click on 'Details" icon so that you can see, on right side what views are extended.  From your post, it seems, the box against Accounting is not ticked

entry.title: Re: Vertical scrollbar jumps to end.

but in mm02 it is not showing

entry.title: Re: Vertical scrollbar jumps to end.

entry.title: Re: Not able to find accounting view in material creation.
entry.content:
hello,

just navigate to the t-code oms2 and find the appropriate material type 

here click on the details which is present in the application toolbar.

 

now you will be navigated to the next screen as below. here just select the accounting view.

save it and now try to create a material with material type.

if the material is already created then just go to mm01 t-code again not mm02 and try to extend the view by entering the material number. Then the views will be populated as above.

Regards,
Neeraj

entry.title: Re: PO Goods Receipt Exception Task Automation in SAP ByD...
entry.content: Hi Thank you very much for taking the time! And you answered me Indeed, as you said, I also thought so and did so I registered my lock in the BEFORE SAVE event And if the logic requires preventing the new OUTBOUND DELIVERY from being saved Then I want to return FALSE And thus prevent the creation of a new OD But I have an error I raise an error message and return FALSE But it is not received But an error is received: Technical error AP_ESI_COMMON 107; report a case Transaction failed; close window and try again Is it possible to understand what the error is Is it necessary to exclude PO in certain statuses? when I raised an error and return FALSE From ConfirmedInboundDelivery The system raised an error But also created InboundDelivery Apparently because of an attempt by the system to update a certain field that is not allowed But I do not understand which field it could be The update I am making is an update to an extension field in the same object Under the same page, that is: node Item { element isExceptionsWarehouse :Indicator; element activity_task_note :MediumText; } not for any backend field related to PO Unless I understand that following the action of confirm inbound delivery updates all sorts of fields in the backend process of the run Do you have any idea which statuses in particular should be excluded?

entry.title: Re: Missing Documents numbers in LO delta extraction
entry.content:
Hi,

Based on my experience with SD, I believe the below may be the issue.

Check notes 178328 & 208010.

In sd userexits, the code works with X & Y
tables, both tables
have to be maintained. What happens usually is that
customers update the X and forget the Y table.

449137 BW extraction SD: Extracting documents without changes
757361 Additional data records in BW when document changed
216448 BW/SIS: Incorrect update / SD user exit
901512 - User exits in Sales Order Processing


When deleting and saving the document, place a breakpoint in the
below programs.
SD Userexits on the application side (Application 11)
MV45AFZZ,
MV45AFZA
MV45AFZB

userexit_save_document_prepare

here the X and Y tables should be filled.

SD Billing Userexits on the application side (Application 13)

RV60AFZZ
RV60AFZA
RV60AFZB

SD Delivery Userexits on the application side (Application 12)

MV50AFZ1 User Exit as of 21D for Delivery Processi
MV50AFZ2 User Exit for Batch Determination
MV50AFZ3 User Exit Delivery Processing for 3.0A Us
MV50AFZK
MV50AFZL
MV50AFZP
MV50AFZT
MV50AFZZ User Exit for Batch Determination

415716 - User exits in delivery processing
901992 - Goods issue posted without being updated in BW

FM called to update queue
MCEX_UPDATE_CALL_11

regards,

Colin

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC automation public cloud Netherlands
entry.content:
Hello Danny,

To test your installed certificate using a test run in SAP for scenarios like VAT return filing or ECL (Electronic Communication with the Tax Authorities), follow these steps:

1. Verify Configuration in SAP:

Ensure the following is properly configured:

  • Communication Arrangement: The communication scenario for VAT or ECL is set up and linked to the correct communication system and user.
  • Certificate Assignment: The installed PKIoverheid certificate is associated with the communication scenario.
  • Endpoint: The endpoint URL provided by the Dutch tax authorities for testing purposes (often different from the production URL) is configured.

2. Access the Test Environment:

  • Obtain the test endpoint URL for the Dutch tax authorities.
    • For VAT returns, this is typically a sandbox environment where no real filing occurs.
    • The endpoint URL is provided in documentation or through communication with the tax authority.
  • Update the endpoint in your communication arrangement to point to the test environment.

3. Perform a Test Submission:

  1. Navigate to the test transaction or app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for the relevant process (e.g., VAT return submission or ECL communication).
  2. Prepare a test payload:
    • Use dummy data or test datasets as specified by the Dutch tax authorities.
    • Avoid using actual sensitive or live data in the test environment.
  3. Trigger the test submission:
    • In SAP, initiate the process that uses the certificate for authentication.
    • For VAT returns, this could involve generating a dummy return file and submitting it.

4. Monitor Communication:

  • Check the Status: Look for success/failure messages in the application log (e.g., in Monitoring Tools under Application Interface Framework or the Communication Monitoring app).
  • Analyze Errors (if any):
    • Review detailed error logs.
    • Common issues include:
      • Certificate not recognized (e.g., wrong format, incorrect chain of trust).
      • Incorrect endpoint configuration.
      • Authentication failure due to mismatched credentials or certificates.

5. Validate Results with the Tax Authority:

  • Contact the Dutch tax authority to confirm successful receipt of the test submission.
  • If errors are found, they may provide guidance on correcting your configuration.

6. Return to Production Setup:

Once the test run is successful:

  1. Update the endpoint in SAP to the production environment URL.
  2. Retest briefly with a live (non-critical) submission, if allowed by the tax authority, to confirm end-to-end functionality.

7. Tools and Logs in SAP:

  • Use Communication Monitoring Tools in SAP Public Cloud to view detailed logs.
  • Navigate to Application Logs in SAP Fiori apps to verify success or troubleshoot errors.

Use above steps for your test run.

If you find my answer satisfactory, please award some points.

Thanks for your appreciation 

Divyesh.

entry.title: Re: Cumulative/YTD load for FI to Group Reporting Integra...
entry.content:

Ashwini Bhor

entry.title: While Adding A/P Invoice from Draft Reconciliation Difference Error
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We are facing an issue in the following scenario:

An A/P Invoice was created based on a GRPO.
There is an existing A/P Down Payment Request that is linked to this invoice.
The A/P Invoice was saved in Draft status.
Now, when the user attempts to add the invoice from the Document Draft Report, the system displays the following error message:

"Reconciliation difference must be zero before reconciling [Message 3821-8]"

Need help in troubleshooting this issue.

entry.title: Re: Custom Header in CPI
entry.content:
Hi,

Can you change to condition as below instead of if (po_number!=null)?

if (po_number)

Also, have you saved the local variable content in the property? In the below script shared by you, you are trying to retrive the value from header instead of property.

Regards,

Priyanka

entry.title: Boosting your experience with the Customer Interaction Center – Get the best from SAP Support
entry.content:
Navigating SAP’s vast support infrastructure can be overwhelming—but that is where the Customer Interaction Center (CIC) steps in as your trusted partner. Whether you are a customer, colleague, or partner, the CIC is here to guide you, connect you with the right teams, and ensure your support journey is smooth and effective.                                       

 
But what exactly do they do, and how can you best interact with them? In this blog post, we will dive into the world of the Customer Interaction Center, helping you become familiar and fully aware of their scope and how they can assist you.

As a proud member of this team, I am excited to walk you through how we can help you get the most out of SAP Support. 

 
Let's explore the key focus areas:

The CIC is your first point of contact for non-product related queries. We help you: 

  • Escalate support cases 
  • Manage S-user ID and Universal ID issues 
  • Navigate SAP for Me 
  • Connect with the right SAP teams 

Let’s break down how we support you in each area. 


Support Case Escalation 

 Have you opened a case with SAP technical support and need further assistance? The Customer Interaction Center is your go-to contact.  
 

Our agents will assist you by sharing a status update of your case and taking the relevant actions to expedite its resolution. 

A few points you should keep in mind:  

  • CIC is not part of the technical team, so we cannot provide the product experts’ advice on your issue.  
  • All requests, including call backs, priority adjustments, and speed-ups, require Business Impact details. This critical information helps prioritize cases.  
  • Ensure Business Impact information is documented in the case or have it ready during your interaction with our agents. 

If you are unsure about the relevant information to share as Business Impact, please refer to these meaningful articles and blog posts with guidance on which details you should consider adding to your case:  

  • What about Business Impact? 
  • SAP Note 1281633 - Speed Up Processing of a Case 
  • SAP Knowledge Base Article 3536459 - Business Impact-Driven Case Prioritization – SAP for Me 
     

S-user ID Support  

Experiencing issues with your S-user ID setup? The Customer Interaction Center is here to help. We can check the status of your ID - whether it is active, expired, or locked - and verify assigned authorizations along with your company’s customer account information. 

We can also identify your Super Administrator's name and suggest the permissions needed for specific accesses in SAP for Me. Remember that our team covers S-user ID use within SAP for Me and SAP Support Portal. Other applications might require expertise from different support areas. 

 
 
SAP for Me Guidance 

SAP for Me is now the central entry for support at SAP. We at CIC are available as a point for non-technical guidance on best practices for using and navigating this portal. 

 

It is important to highlight that our team provides support on customer-facing dashboards like Services & Support and Users & Contacts.  

 

 


 
Dashboards designed for SAP Partners, please contact SAP Partner Support through your SAP Partner Portal for assistance.  
 
When it comes to issues in SAP for Me, the Customer Interaction Center performs an initial troubleshooting focused on your S-user ID status, authorizations, and customer account setup. In case of any technical concerns or data inconsistency happening in the portal, you might be redirected to create a case with SAP for Me technical team, or to interact with another relevant organization that is responsible for the information maintained in the section affected, such as the SAP Contracts team or your company’s SAP Account Manager.  

  • Not sure who your company’s SAP Account Manager is? Connect with us for assistance.  When interacting with our agents, make sure that you are aware of your S-user ID, customer number, and customer name to allow the relevant information to be shared with you.  

    Find more details on SAP Knowledge Base Article 2626344 - Who is the SAP Account Manager/Executive for my company? 
  • What if you need to contact the SAP Order Management (Contracts) team? All you need to do is send your query to them by e-mail at sapcontractcenter@sap.com as referred on SAP Note 1660069 - How to contact SAP Order Management for contract-related questions or issues. 

 
 
Universal ID Assistance 

Lastly, over the past years CIC’s focus has increased on supporting SAP Universal ID users.  
 


Whenever you face an issue or need clarification on setting up your SAP Universal ID, you are encouraged to contact our team.  
 
We are the initial level of support for this type of account and are prepared to confirm your SAP Universal ID status and guide you on best practices or next steps for configuration completion. 
 
For technical issues, our agents may collaborate with the SAP Universal ID technical team for further analysis. 

 
 
In summary, remember that the Customer Interaction Center is your bridge to SAP’s technical organization. We are continuously working to improve your experience and provide you with timely and effective support either by resolving your issue, or by pointing you to the most appropriate channel for your query.  
 
Our team is available 24/7 ensuring you are covered with a “follow the sun” support. With experts located in strategic locations around the world, and service provided in over 10 languages, we are committed to meeting your needs promptly and effectively.  
 
Find the Customer Interaction Center official page and contact information on the following link: Contact Customer Interaction Center  

 

Thank you for reading! We hope you found this helpful and would love to hear your thoughts about this blog post. Let us know what you think!  
 
Stay tuned for our upcoming Support Webinars and recorded sessions to continuously learn and get the best from SAP Support: Register Now 
 

 
Read more #CustomerInteractionCenter blog posts here!

entry.title: SAP Sybase ASE Audit Logs: Handling Multi-Row Single Events in Query Results
entry.content:
How to handle split audit log entries in SAP Sybase ASE?

When querying audit logs in Sybase ASE, I'm facing an issue where the extrainfo column is being split across multiple rows. This makes it difficult to analyse the complete audit information.

Query: 

select * from sybsecurity..sysaudits_01


Example of the issue:
- Row 1: event, eventtime, loginname, dbname, objname, objowner, [part1 of extrainfo...]
- Row 2: event, eventtime, loginname, dbname, objname, objowner,[continuation of extrainfo...]
- Row 3: event, eventtime, loginname, dbname, objname, objowner,[rest of extrainfo...]

I need a way to either:
1. Combine these split rows into a single row per audit event(Reliably)
2. Or increase the character limit for the extrainfo column.

entry.title: Re: Deletion Chart of Account Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @JustinTu,

thank you for reaching out. 

You must create a new chart of accounts, and use this as group chart of accounts from scratch. Please ensure to fill the "Length of G/L Account Number" field, that is 10 digits, before you proceed. 

Please create an incident with SAP using component FI-GL-GL-N-2CL for support with the deletion of the group chart of accounts.

You can find further information in the SAP Help Portal: 

Adapting the Chart of Account to your Needs 

Chart of Accounts Maintenance: Overview  

With kind regards,

Robert

entry.title: Re: Not able to find accounting view in material creation.
entry.content: It could be due to some data inconsistency between MARA and MARC tables.  To correct this, run the report as recommended in SAP note 113966

entry.title: Re: Overview of “Backint for SAP HANA” and FAQ for 3rd Party Backup Tool Support
entry.content:
Good blog - but some of the  links are outdated.

New link to SAP Certified Solutions Directory

entry.title: Re: manifest.json always 404 in launchpad
entry.content:
Hello!

According to the manifest.json you attached, it seems the id of the app is not the same in "sap.app" and "sap.cloud" sections:

 

"sap.app": {
"id": "tes.main",
"type": "application",
"i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
"applicationVersion": {
"version": "0.0.1"
}

"sap.cloud": {
"public": true,
"service": "comsmtesservice"
}

Try using the same id in these sections and deploy the app again.

entry.title: Rework Order Component Validation
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

my requirement is validate the components of rework production order based on parent BOM. if given component is not in the BOM (validate the component) will show error like that.

entry.title: Re: Vertical scrollbar jumps to end.
entry.content: I guess it should be the method ENSURE_NODES_VISIBLE

entry.title: Re: Custom tokens in Onboarding
entry.content:
Hi Piotr,

Yes — Picklist values can come through in Onboarding Process Object mappings, but there are a few key conditions and caveats to be aware of.

  • To Make Picklist Fields Work in Mapping:
    Correct Field Type
  • The field in the Onboarding Process Object must be defined as a Picklist type in the Object Definition (not String or Generic Object).

Example field type: Picklist (String) with a valid picklist assigned.

  1. Picklist Association
  2. The field should be linked to a valid Picklist ID that exists in the system.
  3. Make sure the Picklist is not deprecated or inactive.
  4. Picklist Mapping Destination Supports It

If you’re mapping to Employee Central, Recruiting, or a custom MDF, the destination field must also be a compatible Picklist field.

Direct Mapping Limitations

  1. Direct mapping does not always show picklist fields in the dropdown UI, especially if:
  2. The source or target is dynamic (e.g., another object reference).
  3. The picklist field has no default value or depends on conditional logic.

How to Work Around This:
If direct mapping isn’t showing the picklist field:

Use a Business Rule (via Manage Business Configuration → Data Model Mapping):

Create a rule that sets the value of the picklist field based on a condition.

Example:

plaintext
Copy
Edit
IF process.customRole = "Developer"
THEN set employeeData.jobRole = "DEV_PICKLIST_CODE"

Validate Picklist Values in Manage Data

Check that the picklist code values match exactly, and that the correct external codes are used.

Use “Business Rule for Mapping” option if direct mapping isn’t enough.

Test Case Tip:
Go to Manage Data → Onboarding Process Object, open a record, and manually select a value in your picklist field to ensure it appears and saves correctly.

Then test a full onboarding instance to confirm the mapping carries through.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Bank Country`s are missing in App F6437 Manage Banks...
entry.content: App F6437 Manage Banks - Master Data. In this App you don´t create bank accounts or house banks, instead you create as a first step the bank key. My post is tagged with Public Cloud. THX SB

entry.title: Re: Boosting your experience with the Customer Interaction Center – Get the best from SAP Support
entry.content: Very informative. Interesting to read. Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: Top 10 SAP Cloud ALM News June 2025
entry.content:
Welcome to the June edition of our Top 10 News in SAP Cloud ALM!

This month, we are excited to share a range of innovative updates and enhancements aimed at optimizing your SAP Cloud ALM journey. We have been focused on delivering new features and refining existing ones to support your implementation, operations, and transformation needs more effectively.

Let's explore these updates and see how they can enhance your SAP Cloud ALM experience!

 

 

IMPLEMENTATION UPDATES

1. Enhanced Export Checks with Integrated ATC

The enhanced export check now incorporates the ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) check, allowing you to review your CTS transports in a modifiable status before releasing them from source tenants. This ensures that your code is free from critical errors and adheres to testing process standards. For more details, refer to Transport Checks.

 

2. Mass Edit Story Points in Tasks App

In the Tasks app, you can now mass-edit story points for roadmap tasks, project tasks, and user stories. Additionally, the spreadsheet import functionality is available for mass-editing.

 

3. Advanced Solution Activity Management

In the Solution Activities list, you can now view the Application ID of any applications assigned to a specific solution activity. Additionally, when in Edit mode, you have the capability to directly Assign and Unassign applications for the selected solution activity within this list. As previously available, the application name is an active link that allows you to access the application details directly in the Libraries app.

 

4. SAP Activate Roadmap Selection for Project Creation

When creating a new project in the Guided Implementation app, users now have the option to select an SAP Activate Roadmap, ensuring a more personalized implementation experience.

 

5. Streamlined Transport Checks for your Deployment Schedule

You can now easily perform Downgrade Protection and Cross-Reference Checks on your deployment schedule for a feature bundle. This ensures a smooth and secure deployment process. For more details, check out our guide on Performing Transport Checks on Deployment Schedules.

 

 

OPERATIONS UPDATES

6. Private IP Address Drilldown in Real User Monitoring UI

Introducing a new feature that allows customers to upload a CSV file containing their private IP address ranges and corresponding location details. This function enhances the monitoring UI by enabling a comprehensive drilldown capability for private IP addresses. With this feature, customers gain more detailed insights and greater control over their network monitoring activities.

 

7. Improved Scenario-Level Monitoring Alerts

Scenario-level monitoring alerts keep you informed when scenarios face availability or performance issues across their runners. This feature improves the user experience by delivering comprehensive insights into scenario performance, allowing for timely intervention and enhanced system reliability.

 

8. Integration with SAP Cloud Application Event Hub

Health monitoring now supports SAP Cloud Application Event Hub, enabling seamless integration of event data for comprehensive health analysis. This feature enhances the visibility of system status and potential issues, providing users with more accurate and timely insights for proactive management.

 

9. New Operator for Advanced Event Filtering

A new operator, "Not Contains," has been added to Event Filters, enabling users to refine event parameters more precisely. This addition empowers users to easily exclude specific jobs or automations from their event configurations, thereby streamlining their monitoring processes. For instance, if certain jobs or automations are undesirable when setting up events, users can employ the "Not Contains" operator along with the Job/Automation Name Event filter parameter to exclude them seamlessly. This improvement provides users with greater control and efficiency in managing their monitoring tasks.

 

10. Detailed Replication Status Display

The Business Objects page has been enhanced to provide more comprehensive information about replication instances. The Status Text column in the Business Objects table now displays detailed success or error messages from the application data sent by the managed component. This enhancement offers better contextual insights into the replication process. Additionally, the Business Object Details pane and the Business Object view will show the latest or most critical status text from the managed component, replacing the previous approach of displaying only the status text of the MDI service.

 

Elevate Your Business with SAP Cloud ALM this season and embrace the latest advancements designed to propel your progress and innovation.

Stay tuned for future editions where we’ll continue to bring you the newest innovations and improvements in SAP Cloud ALM and explore the SAP Help Portal!

Embrace the chance to transform your business and explore unprecedented levels of accomplishment. Happy innovating! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Boosting your experience with the Customer Interaction Center – Get the best from SAP Support
entry.content: Very well written and useful content. Thank you for sharing this info!

entry.title: Re: Why does a "Database Login" window appear when using...
entry.content: Must set the Project to X64 and not AnyCPU

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC automation public cloud Netherlands
entry.content:
Hi Chuma,

Is it true that we need both QCert for ESeal and QWAC?
If I look at the trusted providers, only KPN and DigiCert Europe Netherlands can provide both?

I thought KPN would be able to help me, but the person from the helpdesk mentioned digiport private instead of PKIOverheid (which Divyesh mentioned) and doesn't (really) know.

Are there any companies using this already? If yes, I wonder what license they have and where they bought the license.

entry.title: Re: Formatting DateTime Columns in Story Analytics (Repor...
entry.content: Usually, time and date are stored in separate columns—one for each—and may be located in different query tables. They just need to be identified.

entry.title: collate in sap DS trasformations
entry.content:
Hi,
with Sap ds I take data from a Mariadb database with utf8mb4_general_ci collate and using query type transformations I write it to a utf8 text file. But the descriptions that do not undergo transformations are not the same in the final result. It seems that during the process the character collate changes. How can I solve it?
Thanks for your attention
Michela

entry.title: Re: German xRechnung Templates for SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi Kristof,

 

do you have any update on the xslt-template for outgoing invoices? We are also waiting on that.

 

Best regards

Fabian

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio AI units
entry.content: The AI assisted process modeler: text to process feature allows you to convert free text into BPMN diagrams and is using AI units as well.

entry.title: Re: Change subreport properties using .NET SDK
entry.content:
Any subreport parameter beginning with ?PM- are internal objects for CR to use only. Don't play with them.

Create your own subreport parameter and use those for editing etc.

The error is due to the field linked no longer exists.

entry.title: Issue with /SCWM/CHECK_QUANTITY in WT creation after EWM update to S4Core 108
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

we are within the project to update our SAP EWM system to S4Core version 108.

Now we detect some issues regarding the function module: /SCWM/CHECK_QUANTITY

We ran sporadic in the issue, that during the Warehouse Task creation, we got the error in the above function module, that the available  quantity on source storage bin is not enough. 

e.g. in transaction /SCMM/ADHU error message: /SCWM/L3686

e.g. in function module /SCWM/TO_INIT_NEW error message: /SCWM/L3019

Is this issue known by somebody. 

Thank you very much in advance!

Elias

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2025
entry.content:
Join us for the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2025, an essential event for professionals seeking to navigate the complexities of compliance in today's dynamic business environment.

This summit is designed to provide you with a comprehensive understanding of SAP's Document and Reporting Compliance solutions, both current and forthcoming, and how they can be leveraged to enhance your business operations.

The event offers a unique opportunity to provide SAP with your individual feedback, ensuring that your voice is heard in shaping the future of compliance solutions.

Whether you are a seasoned SAP user or new to the platform, this event promises to deliver valuable knowledge and networking opportunities.

Check out the agenda and register here.

What to collaborate with experts on SAP Document and Reporting topics? Join the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance — Early customer engagement Workzone group.

entry.title: Re: S4/HANA - How to develop an SAPUI5 app calling a back...
entry.content: Thanks for the comments. I'll also check the event-driven architecture to see if it applies to my case.

entry.title: Re: oData get_stream with parameters
entry.content: https://mtxhm.sap.tech.it:44300/sap/opu/odata/SAP/ZAPI_MACHINES/machineSet(ID='000112',RefID='QSM54000',PONo='400001545')/$value

entry.title: Re: Custom Header in CPI
entry.content: The empty string and null are not the same thing.

entry.title: Re: getting csv file from AL11 into internal table,appending into custom table end field value have
entry.content: The # will represent the non-displayable character cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline

entry.title: ‌‌‌Online: E-Invoicing and E-Reporting Mandates in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
As France advances towards implementing e-Invoicing and e-Reporting mandates, businesses must stay informed and prepared to ensure compliance. We invite you to join our upcoming session, where SAP experts will provide crucial updates on these mandates and discuss effective timelines. This event will offer valuable insights into the current status and planned release timelines for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, specifically tailored to meet France's regulatory requirements.

Speakers:
Benoît Coutheillas, Localization Product Manager Financial, SAP France
Toan Ha, Area Product Manager, SAP

REGISTER HERE

entry.title: Re: Time difference issue crystal reports
entry.content: In CR Designer hit the F1 key and search for Time, you'll find samples on how to use them in formulae

entry.title: Re: Change subreport properties using .NET SDK
entry.content: Hello Don, for more than 25 years the names of all my parameters have started with Pm-. Is it documented somewhere that I am not allowed to name them like that? And I'm not playing around with the parameters here, in the example code I'm just changing the background color of the subreport. I suspect the problem is that the API does not change the properties of the existing subreport instance but creates a clone, deletes the original and reinserts the clone. This destroys the subreport links. Best regards HML

entry.title: Re: Check the authorization of the user using SAP B1 SDK
entry.content:
Hi,

to Call the Userpermission you should use GetSystemPermission.
GetSystemPermission(UserCode, permKey)

The permKey for 
StockTransfer = 95
GoodsIssue = 94

In older B1 Versions, were an error that you did not receive the effective Permission
regards Lothar

entry.title: Personas EWM RF S/4HANA Public Cloud - custom input field not first field with focus
entry.content:
Hi,

In S4HANA Public Cloud I must add fields and logic to the EWM RF Transaction. I have added a field to a specific screen and this custom field needs to be the first field to get focus when entering the screen. In the screen below, I added the field SerNr where a user will scan a serial number. The product number (and Batch) should then be populated using a logic that I call using this SerNr. value. But the first field is always Prod. and not Sernr. I have to use multiple tabs to get to the correct input field.

Is there a way to change this? In an old blog from 2015 I read about a TabIndex property, but this doesn't exist (anymore?). Before I start trying cumbersome alternatives I need to know that it is not possible to achieve what I want. 

Thank you all in advance,

Ben Meijs

entry.title: Re: SAP Document Reporting Compliance Nigeria
entry.content: Great News, @David2807. We will see more and more legal mandates in e-invoicing in 2025.

entry.title: Re: Current Preparation Plan for C_S4CFI
entry.content:
Hello @Doris_Lindauer, 

Hello,

I have been preparing for the C_S4CFI_2408 certification and noticed that the C_S4CFI_2504 learning path seems to have less content related to fundamental financial topics in SAP compared to previous the version. 

Am I misunderstanding this change and am I missing some of the learning content, or has this reduction been made intentionally by SAP?

Thank you for your clarification.

Best regards,

Benjamin

entry.title: Re: Getting error in SAP Integration Suite IFLOW - Unreso...
entry.content: in the CURL you have header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \ , can you have same in oAuth also

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP Datasphere to SAP RISE
entry.content:
Yes, you need the Data Provisioning Agent and Cloud Connector to fully enable all the connectivity options for your SAP RISE/S4 Hana Connection to Datasphere. Please review your SAP RISE contract, they might be already cloud connector and dpagent ready for configuration.

 

Best Regards,

Leo

entry.title: Re: jurisdiction code
entry.content: My Issue Resolved ,thanks Rohit.

entry.title: Re: Middle Name in SAP Public cloud
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no further updates under the topic after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions. Best regards, Irene

entry.title: Re: Enhance Line Monitor POD with 'Last Indicator Value' Plugin in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Thank you, Manoel, for this insightful article. The Last Indicator Value Plugin seems like a powerful addition to the Line Monitor POD, especially with its real-time updates and customizable appearance. 👏

I have a question: Is it possible to display the latest indicator values in the SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights (DMi) under the "Manage Dashboards" app? For example, similar to how the cycle time chart of the Fanuc injection molding machine is visualized in the Pop-Up Factory in Walldorf? This kind of integration would be incredibly useful for broader operational visibility.

Looking forward to your thoughts 😊
Thanks,

Patrick

entry.title: Re: Problem viewing the entire structure of technical obj...
entry.content: Hi Esnowy, We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no further updates under the topic after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions. Best regards, Irene

entry.title: Re: Enhance a BOPF object generated from a CDS view
entry.content: @JonBlackIs this still the same or does SAP provides an option to enhance custom Action or Validation or determination in Standard BOs created using CDS? Am looking in to enhancing the standard application "Manage change records" which is built on CDS based BOPF.

entry.title: How to link screen-painter to custom table
entry.content:
Hi,

I created an input field using screen painter name 'DATE_AGO' with search helper 'BU_DATE_CHAR', I chose the search helper because I want it like under date format. I have declared in the TOP include a global variable DATA: DATE_AGO TYPE SY-DATUM. 

Now  when I run my project, and I fill that field with date, when I click on enter it automatically clear by it's self, and when I run the debug and I point on the variable name it's empty 00000000.

Here's the affectation of the variable data to the field of my custom table:

 gs_affec_a-date_ago = date_ago.

DATA: DATE_AGO TYPE SY-DATUM.

Please note that I'm not using any clear or something that let the variable be cleared automatically.

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC automation public cloud Netherlands
entry.content:
Hello @Danny5 

Yes, for DRC automation in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, both a QCert for ESeal (for digital signing) and a QWAC (for secure connection) are generally required.

KPN and DigiCert Europe Netherlands can provide these, but it’s important to confirm the certificates comply with PKIOverheid, as required by Dutch tax authorities. Some helpdesks may refer to different products like Digiport Private, so it’s worth double-checking with the provider.

For your reference:

  • Logius PKIOverheid Information

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Re: EFD REINF Erro MS0038
entry.content:
Olá, tudo certo ?

Utilizando o DRC Cloud Edition, a determinação do ambiente de envio depende também da sua subaccount do BTP, pois sua conta pode não estar habilitada para envios produtivos. Por favor, revise se sua conta está realmente habilitada para envios produtivos conforme informado neste link.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) 2505 - Available Now!
entry.content:
Hi Ruchira,

It is planned to have the feature general available with 2511 (subject to change in case of identified issues).

The registration for the early adopter program for 2505 has already ended.

With 2508 we plan to have another early adopter program for one additional feature apart of “Generate Formula”, which is “Generate Formatting”, the registration has not yet started, but is planned to be announced soon.

 

The number of users is not restricted, however customers will need to purchase Joule Premium for Supply Chain which costs 8 AI units per user per month, so that they can use the AI-assisted planning feature in the Excel add-in.

This is relevant for the early adopter program and for general availability.

For details regarding AI in IBP - best is contact Lazra.tozzo@sap.com from my team. 

Regards,

Stephan

entry.title: Webinar: Survey on Digital Transformation (DX) and Artificial Intelligence (AI)
entry.content:
We are excited to announce a unique opportunity for you to share your insights by participating in our survey, conducted in partnership with the Academic Board Ibero-America (ABI).

This survey aims to explore how universities are teaching and researching Digital Transformation (DX) and Artificial Intelligence (AI). We're eager to learn about innovative teaching methods and explore how institutions can work together effectively. 

Why participate?

  • Contribute to shaping future collaborations in DX and AI education.
  • Get a chance to share your innovative teaching methods.
  • Help map out potential opportunities for collaboration between educational institutions.

Your response matters! Please submit your survey responses by July 31st. Rest assured, all data collected will be used solely for this initiative.

We're also excited to announce a webinar on July 2nd at 17:00 (CEST), 9:00 (MEX) where we’ll discuss these topics further and explore ideas together. Join us for this engaging session!

Should you have any questions or need assistance, please reach out to us at jcarrola@novaims.unl.pt 

We look forward to your participation and insights!

entry.title: Re: How does WT quantity rounding work when using the Pac...
entry.content:
Thanks to @Ajit_Routray for pointing me to the right answer. To use rounding correctly, the following assumptions must be present.

The material master must reflect the conversion between PC/CRT/PAL units of measure.
In SCM Extended Warehouse Management->Master Data->Product->Define Unit of Measure Attributes settings, the quantity classification must be assigned to each unit of measure.
There is no need to use a stock-specific unit of measure.

entry.title: load child entity when Create button is clicked in RAP Unmanaged draft based application
entry.content:
I have Root R, Composition child C.

My requirement is that whenever 'create' button at R is clicked, child entries should be automatically created and shown on UI in draft state, rather than after activated.

I do not want to add a new button for loading child entries. I am on SAP S/4HANA on-prem 2023 system. I tried using determinations, side effects. But they did not work.
